programs.name: Federal Investment Tax Credit (ITC)
programs.amount: 30% of total installed cost
programs.description: Dollar-for-dollar federal income tax reduction equal to 30% of the total installed cost of solar, storage, or microgrid systems. Available through 2032 at 30%, stepping down to 26% in 2033 and 22% in 2034. Bonus adders available for domestic content (+10%), energy communities (+10%), and low-income projects (+10-20%).

programs.name: MACRS Accelerated Depreciation
programs.amount: 5-year accelerated depreciation schedule
programs.description: Commercial solar and storage systems can be depreciated over 5 years, with bonus depreciation allowing up to 80% of the depreciable value in the first year. Combined with the 30% ITC, MACRS can reduce effective system cost by 50-60%.

programs.name: IRA Direct Pay (Elective Pay)
programs.amount: 30% direct cash payment
programs.description: Tax-exempt entities (schools, hospitals, governments, nonprofits) can receive the ITC as a direct cash payment instead of a tax credit. Introduced by the Inflation Reduction Act in 2022.

programs.name: USDA REAP Grant
programs.amount: Up to 50% of project cost (25% grant + 25% loan guarantee)
programs.description: Rural Energy for America Program provides grants and loan guarantees to agricultural producers and rural small businesses. Can be combined with the ITC for total incentive coverage of 55-80%.

programs.name: 30C EV Charging Tax Credit
programs.amount: 30% of costs, up to $100,000 per station
programs.description: Federal tax credit for EV charging equipment installed in eligible census tracts. Covers hardware, installation, and electrical upgrades. Extended through 2032 by the Inflation Reduction Act. $1,000 cap for residential installations.

programs.name: NEVI Formula Program
programs.amount: Up to 80% of costs for highway corridor DCFC
programs.description: National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure program distributes $5 billion over 5 years to states for DCFC along highway corridors. Minimum 4 ports of 150 kW each, with 97% uptime requirements.

programs.name: CFI Discretionary Grants
programs.amount: Up to 80% of costs
programs.description: Competitive federal grant program for EV charging projects in community locations. $2.5 billion total over 5 years.

programs.name: Self-Generation Incentive Program (SGIP)
programs.amount: $200-$1,000 per kWh of storage capacity
programs.description: California rebate program for energy storage systems. General market incentive is approximately $200/kWh. Equity categories receive $850-$1,000/kWh, which can cover 85-100% of battery system cost.

programs.name: Net Billing Tariff (NEM 3.0)
programs.amount: Export rate varies by time of day and utility
programs.description: California's updated net metering policy pays reduced export rates but strongly incentivizes battery storage to maximize self-consumption and shift exports to peak hours.

programs.name: CALeVIP EV Charging Rebates
programs.amount: Up to $80,000 per DCFC, $6,500 per L2
programs.description: California Electric Vehicle Infrastructure Project provides rebates for EV charger installations at workplaces, multi-unit dwellings, and public locations. Funding varies by region.

programs.name: California Solar Property Tax Exclusion
programs.amount: 100% property tax exclusion for solar
programs.description: Solar energy systems are excluded from property tax assessment in California, meaning your property taxes will not increase after installing solar panels or battery storage.

programs.name: NY-Sun Incentive Program
programs.amount: $0.20 - $0.80/W depending on sector and region
programs.description: NYSERDA solar incentive program providing per-watt rebates for residential and commercial solar installations. Incentive levels vary by utility territory and project size.

programs.name: Value of Distributed Energy Resources (VDER)
programs.amount: Location-based export credits
programs.description: New York's value stack tariff compensates solar exports based on energy, capacity, environmental, and demand reduction values. Rates vary by utility and location.

programs.name: Drive Clean Rebate
programs.amount: Up to $4,000 per L2 port (commercial)
programs.description: NYSERDA rebate for Level 2 EV charger installations at workplaces, multi-unit dwellings, and public parking facilities.

programs.name: Illinois Adjustable Block Program
programs.amount: 15-year SREC contract (value varies by block)
programs.description: Illinois Shines program provides Solar Renewable Energy Credits (SRECs) through 15-year contracts. Residential systems under 25 kW receive upfront payment based on estimated production.

programs.name: CEJA EV Charging Rebates
programs.amount: Up to 80% of costs in disadvantaged communities
programs.description: Climate and Equitable Jobs Act provides EV charging rebates prioritizing disadvantaged communities. Covers hardware, installation, and electrical upgrades.

programs.name: Massachusetts SMART Program
programs.amount: Performance-based incentive (per kWh generated)
programs.description: Solar Massachusetts Renewable Target (SMART) provides performance-based incentives paid over 10 or 20 years. Storage adder provides additional per-kWh incentive for paired storage.

programs.name: Texas Solar Property Tax Exemption
programs.amount: 100% property tax exemption for solar value
programs.description: Texas exempts the appraised value added by solar energy systems from property taxes. This means installing solar will not increase your property tax bill.

programs.name: Xcel Energy Solar*Rewards
programs.amount: $0.02-$0.03/kWh REC payments for 20 years
programs.description: Xcel Energy purchases Renewable Energy Credits from residential and commercial solar systems through 20-year contracts, providing ongoing production-based payments.

programs.name: Colorado Charge Ahead Rebates
programs.description: Colorado provides rebates for EV charger installations at workplaces, multi-unit dwellings, and public facilities. Higher incentives for disadvantaged communities.

programs.name: APS Solar Incentive Program
programs.amount: $0.10/kWh export credit + demand charge reduction
programs.description: Arizona Public Service provides solar export credits and reduced demand charges for customers with solar and battery storage systems.

programs.name: New Jersey SREC-II (Successor)
programs.amount: Fixed 15-year SREC price (varies by project class)
programs.description: New Jersey's Successor Solar Incentive program provides fixed-price SRECs through the Administratively Determined Incentive (ADI) and competitive solicitation programs.

programs.name: New Jersey Storage Incentive Program
programs.amount: $150-$300 per kWh of storage capacity
programs.description: New Jersey provides per-kWh incentives for behind-the-meter energy storage systems, with bonus incentives for projects in overburdened communities.

programs.name: Florida Net Metering
programs.amount: Full retail rate credit for excess generation
programs.description: Florida utilities provide full retail rate net metering credits for solar energy exported to the grid. Systems up to 2 MW are eligible. Credits carry forward monthly and are paid annually.

programs.name: Florida Solar Property Tax Exemption
programs.amount: 100% property tax exemption for residential solar
programs.description: Residential solar energy systems are exempt from property tax assessment in Florida, and solar equipment is exempt from state sales tax.

programs.name: State Utility EV Rebates (Various)
programs.amount: $500-$4,000 per L2 port, up to $25,000 per DCFC
programs.description: Many electric utilities offer rebates for EV charger installations. Major programs include Pacific Gas & Electric (CA), Duke Energy (NC, SC, FL), and Con Edison (NY). Contact your local utility directly.

programs.name: ZEVIP (Zero Emission Vehicle Infrastructure)
programs.amount: Up to 50% of costs, max $5,000/L2 or $50,000/DCFC
programs.description: Natural Resources Canada program supporting EV charger installation at workplaces, multi-unit residential buildings, public places, and fleet depots. Multiple intake rounds per year.

programs.name: Canada Infrastructure Bank - Green Infrastructure
programs.amount: Low-cost financing and co-investment
programs.description: The Canada Infrastructure Bank provides low-cost financing for large-scale renewable energy and energy storage projects. Indigenous community projects receive priority.

programs.name: Canada Greener Homes Initiative
programs.amount: Up to $5,000 for solar PV
programs.description: Federal grant program for residential energy efficiency and renewable energy upgrades. Requires EnerGuide evaluation before and after installation.

programs.name: iMHZEV Fleet Incentive
programs.amount: $5,000 per L2 port for medium/heavy-duty fleet
programs.description: Incentives for Medium- and Heavy-duty Zero-Emission Vehicles program supports fleet electrification including charging infrastructure for commercial and transit fleets.

programs.name: Save on Energy Programs
programs.amount: Various incentives for commercial energy projects
programs.description: Ontario's IESO-funded Save on Energy programs provide commercial and industrial incentives for clean energy projects including solar and storage installations.

programs.name: Ontario EV Charger Grant Program
programs.amount: Up to $2,000 per connector for condos and workplaces
programs.description: Ontario provides grants for EV charger installations at condominiums and workplaces. Supports Level 2 charging infrastructure deployment.

programs.name: CleanBC Better Homes Program
programs.amount: Up to $5,000 for solar, $2,000 for EV chargers
programs.description: BC provides rebates for home energy improvements including solar installations and EV charger connections. Enhanced rebates for income-qualified households.

programs.name: BC Hydro Commercial EV Charging
programs.amount: Up to $4,000 per L2 connector + $600 electrical
programs.description: BC Hydro provides rebates for commercial and multi-unit residential EV charging installations, covering hardware and electrical upgrade costs.

programs.name: Roulez vert Program
programs.amount: Up to $5,000 per EV connector
programs.description: Quebec's EV incentive program provides rebates for EV charging infrastructure at homes, workplaces, and multi-unit buildings. One of Canada's most generous provincial EV programs.

programs.name: Chauffez vert Program
programs.amount: Up to $5,000 for renewable heating conversion
programs.description: Quebec rebate program supporting conversion from fossil fuel heating to renewable energy sources including solar thermal and heat pump systems.

programs.name: Municipal Climate Change Action Centre
programs.amount: Varies by project and municipality
programs.description: MCCAC provides funding support for Alberta municipalities undertaking clean energy projects including solar installations, battery storage, and EV charging infrastructure.

programs.name: Alberta Micro-generation Program
programs.amount: Credit at retail electricity rate for exports
programs.description: Alberta's micro-generation regulation allows solar system owners to receive credits at the retail rate for electricity exported to the grid. Systems up to 5 MW are eligible.

programs.name: SaskPower Net Metering
programs.amount: Credit at retail rate, up to 100 kW
programs.description: SaskPower provides net metering for renewable energy systems up to 100 kW. Export credits at the retail electricity rate, carried forward for up to 12 months.

programs.name: Efficiency Manitoba Solar Rebate
programs.amount: Custom rebate based on system performance
programs.description: Efficiency Manitoba offers rebates for solar photovoltaic installations, with incentive amounts that vary based on projected energy production and system specifications.

programs.name: SolarHomes Program
programs.amount: Up to $3,000 for residential solar
programs.description: Nova Scotia provides rebates for residential solar PV installations through the SolarHomes program, supporting homeowners in reducing electricity costs.

programs.name: NB Power Solar Program
programs.amount: Net metering credits at retail rate
programs.description: NB Power offers net metering for solar systems up to 100 kW. Credits are applied monthly at the retail electricity rate, with annual settlement.
